rulings.Case Title: ASMAHAN ATTAYEB VS. MATTHEW C. MULLENWEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To 1) Form Interrogatories – Employment Law, 2) Form Interrogatories – General; 3) Requests For Production; 4) Requests For Admission And (5) For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 28, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF ASMAHAN ATTAYEB AN INDIVIDUAL Motion In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To 1) Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, 2) Form Interrogatories - General; 3) Requests For Production; 4) Requests For Admission And (5) For Sanctions Plaintiff Asmahan Attayeb's Motion to Compel Further Responses to 1) Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, 2) Form Interrogatories - General; 3) Requests for Production; and 4) Requests for Admission; and for Sanctions, is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: METROPOLITAN ELECTRICAL CONSTRUCTION, INC, VS. SUTTER HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Paul A. Herp; Declaration Of Richard G. Cleveland; [Proposed] Order (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 28, 2025, line 1, 2 - DEFENDANT BAYVIEW ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC. Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default The opposed motion is granted. "It is the policy of the law to favor, wherever possible, a hearing on the merits . . . ." (Murray & Murray v. Raissi Real Estate Development, LLC (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 379, 388 [citing Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 854].) Here, Bayview has presented evidence to rebut the presumption of proper service established by Evidence Code 647, leaving it open for this court to weigh the evidence of service presented by the parties. In any event, relief is proper under CCP 473(b). Bayview has acted diligently since learning of the default and Metropolitan presents no evidence of prejudice. Bayview's default is set aside. This defendant shall answer or otherwise respond to the complaint within 20 days of entry of this order. Counsel for Metropolitan failed to provide courtesy copies of filed briefs as required by SFLR 2.7B. This matter was previously on calendar on December 27, 2024 but was continued for failure to provide courtesy copies. Metropolitan is ordered to show cause why it should not be sanctioned in the amount of $250 for its failure to follow SFLR 2.7B. The OSC will be set for hearing on February 25, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301 of this court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)
